source_locators: RFC 9935 section 3, extracted-text lines 174-189: ML-KEM public-key AlgorithmIdentifier OIDs and absent parameters.
RFC 9935 section 4, extracted-text lines 221-240: ML-KEM public-key ASN.1 definitions with PARAMS ARE absent.
requirements.requirement: ML-KEM SubjectPublicKeyInfo AlgorithmIdentifier must use an ML-KEM OID and absent parameters.
requirements.justification: Malformed ML-KEM AlgorithmIdentifier breaks PKIX interoperability at issuance and consumption time.

source_locators: RFC 9935 section 5, extracted-text lines 277-280: keyEncipherment MUST be the only key usage for certificates with id-alg-ml-kem-*.
requirements.requirement: If keyUsage is present for an ML-KEM certificate, keyEncipherment must be the only active bit.
requirements.justification: ML-KEM certificate keyUsage is an issuance policy requirement and should block misprofiled certificates.

source_locators: FIPS 203 section 8 Table 3, extracted-text lines 2139-2144: ML-KEM encapsulation key sizes 800, 1184, and 1568 bytes.
RFC 9935 section 4, extracted-text lines 244-248: ML-KEM PublicKey OCTET STRING sizes 800, 1184, and 1568 bytes.
requirements.requirement: ML-KEM public key payload length must match the parameter set: 800, 1184, or 1568 bytes.
requirements.justification: Wrong ML-KEM public-key length is a structural defect that indicates malformed SPKI encoding.

source_locators: FIPS 203 section 4, extracted-text lines 1286-1297: ByteDecode12 can produce values that cannot occur from ByteEncode12.
FIPS 203 section 7.2, extracted-text lines 2028-2040: encapsulation-key type and modulus checks using ByteEncode12(ByteDecode12(...)).
requirements.requirement: Decoding and then re-encoding the ML-KEM encapsulation key must reproduce the original byte string.
requirements.justification: Unreduced ML-KEM encoded keys are structurally suspicious and should at least surface operationally until local detection is implemented.

source_locators: RFC 9935 section 6, extracted-text lines 329-356: ML-KEM PrivateKey CHOICE seed [0] OCTET STRING size 64 for all parameter sets.
RFC 9935 section 6, extracted-text lines 365-399: seed format and ASN.1 tag parsing guidance.
requirements.requirement: ML-KEM private-key seed representation must be 64 bytes.
requirements.justification: ML-KEM seed-only private keys must match the RFC container size before import proceeds.

source_locators: RFC 9935 section 6, extracted-text lines 335-356: expandedKey sizes 1632, 2400, and 3168 bytes.
FIPS 203 section 8 Table 3, extracted-text lines 2139-2144: decapsulation key sizes 1632, 2400, and 3168 bytes.
requirements.requirement: ML-KEM expanded private key length must match the parameter set: 1632, 2400, or 3168 bytes.
requirements.justification: ML-KEM expanded private keys must match parameter-set sizes before import proceeds.

source_locators: RFC 9935 section 8, extracted-text lines 427-432: recipient should check seed/expandedKey consistency and MUST reject if the check fails.
requirements.requirement: If an ML-KEM private key contains both seed and expanded key, import validation should regenerate and reject malformed inconsistency.
requirements.justification: A both-form ML-KEM private key must be rejected on seed/expanded mismatch during import.

source_locators: FIPS 203 section 7.3, extracted-text lines 2073-2088: decapsulation input hash check before Decaps.
RFC 9935 section 8, extracted-text lines 443-445: expandedKey requires FIPS 203 hash check before use.
requirements.requirement: An ML-KEM expanded private key requires the relevant import hash check before use.
requirements.justification: Expanded ML-KEM private keys require the FIPS/RFC hash check before use.

source_locators: RFC 9881 section 2, extracted-text lines 179-189: ML-DSA OIDs and absent parameters.
RFC 9881 section 4, extracted-text lines 336-350: ML-DSA public-key ASN.1 definitions.
requirements.requirement: ML-DSA SubjectPublicKeyInfo AlgorithmIdentifier must use an ML-DSA OID and absent parameters.
requirements.justification: Malformed ML-DSA AlgorithmIdentifier breaks PKIX interoperability at issuance and validation time.

source_locators: RFC 9881 section 3, extracted-text lines 246-272: ML-DSA signature AlgorithmIdentifier definitions and absent parameters.
requirements.requirement: ML-DSA signatureAlgorithm must use an ML-DSA OID with absent parameters.
requirements.justification: ML-DSA certificate signatureAlgorithm encoding is a PKIX conformance requirement and should block issuance defects.

source_locators: RFC 9881 section 5, extracted-text lines 381-396: if keyUsage is present for ML-DSA, at least one signing-related bit is required.
requirements.requirement: If keyUsage is present for an ML-DSA certificate, at least one of digitalSignature, nonRepudiation, keyCertSign, or cRLSign must be active.
requirements.justification: ML-DSA certificates need a permitted signing usage when keyUsage is present.

source_locators: RFC 9881 section 5, extracted-text lines 404-413: ML-DSA keyUsage MUST NOT contain key-establishment or encryption bits.
requirements.requirement: ML-DSA keyUsage must not contain keyEncipherment, dataEncipherment, keyAgreement, encipherOnly, or decipherOnly.
requirements.justification: ML-DSA certificates must not advertise encipherment or agreement semantics they cannot safely support.

source_locators: FIPS 204 section 4 Table 2, extracted-text lines 1105-1110: ML-DSA public key sizes 1312, 1952, and 2592 bytes.
RFC 9881 section 4, extracted-text lines 356-360: ML-DSA PublicKey OCTET STRING sizes 1312, 1952, and 2592 bytes.
RFC 9881 Appendix A, extracted-text lines 895-899: known appendix transcription conflict for ML-DSA-87-PublicKey as 2602.
requirements.requirement: ML-DSA public key payload length must match the parameter set: 1312, 1952, or 2592 bytes.
requirements.justification: Wrong ML-DSA public-key length is a structural defect that indicates malformed SPKI encoding.

source_locators: RFC 9881 section 8.3, extracted-text lines 600-611: HashML-DSA MUST NOT be used in the listed PKIX certificate, CRL, OCSP, and related contexts.
FIPS 204 section 5.4, extracted-text lines 1218-1231: HashML-DSA is pre-hash ML-DSA and requires identifiers/application handling distinct from pure ML-DSA.
requirements.requirement: HashML-DSA must not be used for PKIX certificates, CRLs, OCSP, issuance, or related protocols where RFC 9881 prohibits it.
requirements.justification: HashML-DSA is prohibited in the covered PKIX certificate contexts and should always block.

source_locators: RFC 9881 section 6, extracted-text lines 459-496: ML-DSA PrivateKey CHOICE seed [0] OCTET STRING size 32 for all parameter sets.
RFC 9881 section 6, extracted-text lines 507-540: seed format and ASN.1 tag parsing guidance.
requirements.requirement: ML-DSA private-key seed representation must be 32 bytes.
requirements.justification: ML-DSA seed-only private keys must match the RFC container size before import proceeds.

source_locators: RFC 9881 section 6, extracted-text lines 473-496: expandedKey sizes 2560, 4032, and 4896 bytes.
FIPS 204 section 4 Table 2, extracted-text lines 1105-1110: ML-DSA private key sizes 2560, 4032, and 4896 bytes.
requirements.requirement: ML-DSA expanded private key length must match the parameter set: 2560, 4032, or 4896 bytes.
requirements.justification: ML-DSA expanded private keys must match parameter-set sizes before import proceeds.

requirements.requirement: If an ML-DSA private key contains both seed and expanded key, import validation should regenerate and reject malformed inconsistency.
requirements.justification: A both-form ML-DSA private key must be rejected on seed/expanded mismatch during import.
